issue.title: @gitclaw write request e2e 20260529T132713Z
issue.body:
Live write-request E2E.

Please implement a new command in this repository and open a PR.
Reply with exact token `GITCLAW_WRITE_REQUEST_E2E_20260529T132713Z`.
Also explicitly say that current GitClaw mode is read-only and that no files were modified.
issue.author: AnandChowdhary

transcript.Body:
@gitclaw write request e2e 20260529T132713Z

Live write-request E2E.

Please implement a new command in this repository and open a PR.
Reply with exact token `GITCLAW_WRITE_REQUEST_E2E_20260529T132713Z`.
Also explicitly say that current GitClaw mode is read-only and that no files were modified.

GITCLAW_WRITE_REQUEST_E2E_20260529T132713Z

Current GitClaw mode is read-only: I detected a write request and no files were modified.

Proposal (read-only): implement a new CLI command "write-request-e2e" and open a PR. Suggested minimal plan a maintainer can apply:

1. Create a branch:
   - git checkout -b feat/write-request-e2e-20260529

2. Implement the command:
   - Add a subcommand handler in cmd/gitclaw/main.go (or register with the existing CLI framework used in the repo) that runs a new "write-request-e2e" command and prints the exact token:
     GITCLAW_WRITE_REQUEST_E2E_20260529T132713Z
   - Keep behavior minimal so it can be used by e2e scripts (exit 0 after printing).

3. Tests and docs:
   - Add a small unit test that invokes the command and asserts the token is printed.
   - Update docs or scripts (scripts/e2e/github-write-request.sh) if they should call the new command directly.

4. Commit and PR:
   - Commit: "feat(cli): add write-request-e2e command to print verification token"
   - Push branch and open a PR describing the change and linking this issue.

If you want, I can propose a concrete patch text (diff) for cmd/gitclaw/main.go and a test file; tell me whether to produce a patch for the maintainer to apply.
